author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
There is No Reason for Self-Conceit.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Foolish and inconsiderate men, who have neither wisdom nor instruction, mock and deride us, being eager to exalt themselves in their own conceits.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For what can a mortal man do, or what strength is there in one made out of the dust?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For it is written, “There was no shape before mine eyes, only I heard a sound, and a voice [saying], What then?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Shall a man be pure before the Lord?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Or shall such an one be [counted] blameless in his deeds, seeing He does not confide in His servants, and has charged even His angels with perversity?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The heaven is not clean in His sight: how much less they that dwell in houses of clay, of which also we ourselves were made!

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: He smote them as a moth; and from morning even until evening they endure not.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Because they could furnish no assistance to themselves, they perished.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: He breathed upon them, and they died, because they had no wisdom.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: But call now, if any one will answer thee, or if thou wilt look to any of the holy angels; for wrath destroys the foolish man, and envy killeth him that is in error.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: I have seen the foolish taking root, but their habitation was presently consumed.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let their sons be far from safety; let them be despised before the gates of those less than themselves, and there shall be none to deliver.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For what was prepared for them, the righteous shall eat; and they shall not be delivered from evil.”
